data.narrative:
Anyon Systems is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised C$23M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies across public and private markets: 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing comp universe contains only 7 same-sector peers total, and filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yields fewer than three datapoints—below the minimum threshold for reliable median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and obscure sector-specific dynamics. Consequently, this analysis cannot anchor to a statistically defensible peer multiple.

A credible valuation range for Anyon Systems cannot be derived from the available data using standard triangulation methods. The sector remains highly heterogeneous in stage, technology approach, and capitalization structure, limiting comparability. Valuation determination would require either: (1) expansion of the comp set through private transaction data or contemporaneous funding rounds; (2) DCF or scenario-based analysis incorporating development timelines and market adoption assumptions; or (3) direct reference to recent seed or Series A pricing for quantum hardware or software subsegments. Without one of these additional inputs, any numerical valuation would be speculative rather than analytically grounded.
